titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Nicolas Nabokov Papers
titlestmt.author: by Karen Spicher and Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12230196.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 04:08

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In French, Russian, English, and German.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Dominique Nabokov on the Edith and Richard French Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2004 and 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: I: 2004 Acquisitions, 1920-1999. II. September 2016 Acquisition, circa 1950-1978.

bioghist.head: Nicolas Nabokov (1903-1978)
bioghist.p: Nicolas Nabokov was a Russian-born composer and writer. He was first cousin of Russian-American author Vladimir Nabokov. Nabokov was married four times, the last of which was to photographer Dominique Nabokov. He taught at numerous institions including Wells College and the Peabody Conservatory. Nabokov also organized music and cultural festivals in his role as the Secretary General of the Congress for Cultural Freedom.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Nicolas Nabokov Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Former call numbers: Uncat Mss 618 and Uncat Mss 716. In 2014, the collection received a baseline level of processing, including partial rehousing and minimal organization. The collection was reclassed under one call number, materials were organized into series, and containers were renumbered in a single sequence. Most folder titles were based on information provided by the creator or vendor, while some were assigned by Library staff. Folder titles were not verified against contents.
Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, musical scores, writings, datebooks, research, personal papers, ephemera, and other papers by or relating to the life and work of Nicolas Nabokov.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Nicolas Nabokov Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Dominique Nabokov on the Edith and Richard French Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2004.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two series: I. Correspondence, 1928-1980. II. Music and Writings, 1927-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, musical scores, and writings, along with personal papers and other papers by or relating to Nicolas Nabokov's life and work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or subject, followed by unsorted

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence with musicians including Pierre Boulez, Paul Collaer, Roger Désormière, Wilhelm Furtwängler, Aram Khachaturian, Lina Prokofiev, Igor Markevitch, Igor Stravinsky, and Virgil Thomson.
Other correspondents include Vladimir Nabokov and members of the Nabokov family.

did.unittitle: Hofmannsthal, Raimund von, and Hofmannsthal, Elizabeth von
Morris, Brewster H., and Morris, Helen
Nabokov, Vladimir; Nabokova, Vera; and Nabokov, Dmitri
New York City Ballet, and Cage, Betty
Rothschild, Philippe, baron de, and Rothschild, Pauline, baroness de
Stravinsky festival (Paris and Berlin, 1980) (notes and printed material)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains full and vocal scores, libretti, correspondence, and printed materials relating to other ballets, oratorios, and operas. Also contains extensive drafts and manuscripts for writing projects including Nabokov's memoirs and writings about Igor Stravinsky.

did.unittitle: Bagázh: Memoirs of a Russian Cosmopolitan (memoir)
Don Quixote (ballet; libretto by Nabokov and George Balanchine)
Synopsis and libretto (manuscript and typescript), and photographs
"The Gracious Master" (biography of Igor Stravinsky). Typescript, with revisions
Job (oratorio; libretto by Jacques Maritain). Libretto fragments, correspondence, and printed material
The Last Flower (ballet; after James Thurber). Manuscript music fragment and correspondence
Love's Labour's Lost (opera; libretto by W. H. Auden and Chester Kallman, after William Shakespeare)
Related writings by Nabokov and Kallmann, reviews, and other printed material
The Nutcracker (film scenario by Nabokov and George Balanchine, after E. T. A. Hoffmann)
Ode: Méditation sur la majesté de Dieu (ballet-oratorio; libretto by Roger Désormière, after Mikhail Vasilʹevich Lomonosov)
Vocal score (printed, Editions Maurice Senart). Photocopy
Paul et Virginie (opera; libretto by Jean Cocteau and Raymond Radiguet). Libretto, typescript photocopy
Der Tod des Grigorij Rasputin (opera; libretto by Nabokov and Stephen Spender)
Libretto in English, by Spender (autograph manuscript and typescript)
Libretto and set design, in English, Act I (typescript carbon)
"A Partisan Chronicle" (about Igor Stravinsky). Typescript and autograph manuscript
"The Peasant Marriage" (notes for lecture about Les noces , by Igor Stravinsky,). Typescript photocopy, with revisions
Variations on a Theme by Tchaikovsky (cello and orchestra). Printed program from performance

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Dominique Nabokov on the Edith and Richard French Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, photographs, printed material, personal papers, and other papers by or relating to Nicolas Nabokov.

did.unittitle: Papers related to Celeste and Armand Bartos
Congress of Cultural Freedom: Mary West
Vincent Groud manuscripts related to Nabokov project
Papers related to Dimitri Nicolaevich Nabokov
Correspondence related to N. Nabokov's death
Vladimir D. Nabokov correspondence and printed material
Correspondence related to New York Public Library
"Alkestis in Monte Carlo" opera scenario by George Steiner
Stravinsky Festival in Paris and Berlin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Robert Gottlieb; Robert Craft; and Eva Resnikova.

did.unittitle: "From the beaches to Berlin, a March" musical score
"Trois Poemes of Stephane Mallarme" musical score by Maurice Ravel
"Interludium Between Scene III and IV (night scene)" musical score and printed material
Orchestra suite: Goldberg, Bach, Partition Calques
"Chants a la Vierge Marie" musical score
"Concerto per pianoforte in C" musical score
"Serenata Estiva (Summer Serenata) for String Quartet" musical score
"Biblical Symphony or Symphony No. II" musical score
Musical scores for "Quatre Poemes de Boris Pasternak" and others with related correspondence

News articles concerning Dmitri Shostakovitch and manuscript drafts for "Music and Peace," "Communist Sponsorship of Waldorf Meeting Unmasked," and "Joint Statement on the Cultural and Scientific Conference"
Photograph of Sophie Nabokov and Victor Fasolt
Posters for Bryn Mawr college lectures
Ordre National de La Legion D'Honneur certificate with related correspondence
Musical scores for "Six Variations and Finale," "Praeludium, 4 Variations and Finale," and "Praeludium"
Dimitri N. Nabokov--criticism in Polish newspapers
News articles relating to Nicolas Nabokov
"Don Quixote" playbills, reviews, press clippings, and related correspondence
"Rasputin" press clippings and photocopies of related correspondence
"Palais de Monaco" featuring "Creation en Premiere Mondiale" by Nicolas Nabokov programs
Magazines with articles on Nicolas Nabokov and Igor Stravinsky
Institute of Arts and Letters membership for Nicolas Nabokov
Pencil sketch portrait of unidentified woman
Personal papers including "Certificat d'Identite" and correspondence from U.S. Department of Labor: Immigration and Naturalization Service
Personal papers including photocopied translation of correspondence with Social Security Headquarters
Nabokov family photographs of Vladimir, Vera, and Serge
"The last view of Yalta's Harbor" photograph by Nicolas Nabokov's mother
Photographs from the memoirs of Nicolas Nabokov, German edition, "Zwei Rechte Shuhe im Gepack"
Photographs of Nicolas Nabokov's grandparents and uncle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Nicolas Nabokov and his friends including Charles Thyer, Isaiah Berlin, Seonid Berman, Eliott Carter and Helen Carter, Nina Berbers, Helena Rubinsteen, James Roosevelt, Patricia Blake, Francis Poulenc, Grisha Piatigorsky, Arthur Honegger, Eugene Berman, Virgil Thomson, Euggene Ormandy, H.H. Stuckenschmidt, Stuart Hampshire, Elizabeth von Hofimannstahl, Prince Bernhard of Sippe, Obey Prokofiev; Heinrich Strobel, Alain Danielou, Boris Blache, Jacques Bornoff, and Laurence Olivier.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Nicolas Nabokov with friends, collaborators, and musicians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Nicolas Nabokov and others including Stephen Spender, George Balanchine, M. Rostropovitch, Rubinstein family, Igor Stravensky, Vera Stravinsky, Dominique Nabokov, Rolf Lieberman, Denise Daval, Mary McCarthy, Yehush Menuhin, Aribert Reiman, Boris Blacher, Laurence Olivier, and George Skiline.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Nicolas Nabokov with Dominique Nabokov
Nicolas Nabokov and Dominique Nabokov wedding photograph
"Societe des auteuis compositeurs et Editeurs de Mosique" poster
"And It Was So (An Autobiographical Chronicle)" manuscript
"And It Was So (An Autobiographical Chronicle)" and "Stories Part I: Childhood" manuscripts
"Part IV America de Beautiful" of "And It Was So" edited manuscript
"Wystan Auden" typed and handwritten manuscripts related to "Love's Labor's Lost"
"Bagasz places, houses, friends: a memoir" Part I edited manuscript
"Bagasz places, houses, friends: a memoir" Part II edited manuscript
"Bagasz places, houses, friends: a memoir" Part III complete edited manuscript
"On the Wings of the C.J.A." typed and handwritten manuscripts with annotations and related correspondence
"The Gracious Master" edited typescript manuscript with holograph page
"Troductions de A. Pouchkine par Leonid Berman" handwritten manuscript with notes
"The 10 days that stretched the world" manuscript in Russian with handwritten note in French
"Nicolas Nabokov's 'Love's Labor's Lost': A Study of its Origins, Libretto, and Musical Style" manuscript by Shari Suzanne Kolding with related research
"Love's Labor Lost" play manuscript with related correspondence
"Love's Labor's Lost" play manuscript with related articles
Book ideas with related letters and photographs
"Part II Adolescence and Onward" of "It Was So" edited manuscript
Autobiography Part III "The Twenties in Germany" manuscript
"Bagasz places, houses, friends: a memoir" typescript and holograph partial manuscripts
"Alexis de Saintleger Leger" holograph manuscript, "Le Secretaire General" typescript manuscript, and research
Personal writing, including "College Exile" and "Games" typescript manuscripts and untitled holograph manuscript
"The All of Me (an autobiographical chronicle)" edited manuscript
Nicolas Nabokov's curriculum vitae and discographies
Edited manuscript for "Part I: Childhood" from "A Life Uninvented: An Autobiographical Chronicle and Stories"
"The Gracious Master" edited manuscript and "Meet Rostropovitch" unedited manuscript
"College Exile I" edited holograph manuscript with related correspondence
Handwritten manuscripts for" Fruhlingserwachen," "Back to 1917," and "Teydoora and the Loved Ones" with one untitled partial typed manuscript
Edited, handwritten manuscripts for "Arrivals in Aurora," "To America and to Union Pacific," and "Cousin Vladimir"
Edited, handwritten manuscripts for "1917," "The birth of an opera," and "From Louisville to Europe"
Untitled holograph manuscript pages and holograph notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of "The Interval," "In the Diaghilev Circuit," "The Mimsky Friday," and "Vigil in Aurora."

did.unittitle: "Bobok in Weimar" edited typescript and handwritten manuscripts in German, with related research and correspondence
Edited, handwritten manuscripts for "The Beautiful People," "The Waldorf-Astoria Conference," and "The Ad-Hook Committee"
"Svadebka" edited holograph manuscript and holograph notes
"The Golden Age" typescript and handwritten manuscripts with "Ages of Lives" book outline
"Diaghilev and His Sorcerer Apprentice" edited manuscript with handwritten manuscript pages and notes
Untitled book holograph notes and research
"Occupational Occupations" manuscripts, untitled manuscript pages, and research
"The Buddhist Marriage" holograph manuscripts, untitled manuscripts, and research papers
"The D.P. Camp Interlude" typescript manuscript with research
Writings for New York Review of Books
Outline of Memoirs II: manuscripts for "Alexis de Saintleger Leger" and "The casier judiciaire and the FBI" with related research
"A Short Biographical Sketch of Igor Stravinsky" unedited manuscript and "Igor Stravinsky: A Partisan Chronicle" edited manuscript
"A Short Biographical Sketch of Igor Stravinsky" edited manuscript
"Igor Stravinsky: A Partisan Chronicle" edited manuscript, holograph manuscript pages, and holograph notes
"The Lady and the Unicorn: The Tapestries of the Cluny Museum" edited manuscript, holograph notes, research, and slides
